method_atom: A technique for exactly computing the probabilities of local discrete state-variables in a critical lattice system within a bounded geometry (a half-plane with either absorbing or reflecting boundary conditions), then matching their continuum scaling limit to the operators of a logarithmic conformal field theory at a fixed central charge. The matching assigns each observable to a primary field or its logarithmic partner with a definite scaling dimension, yields finite-size corrections checkable against numerics, and lets one conjecture the scaling form of two-point correlation functions.
object_atom: The four height variables (values 1 to 4) of the two-dimensional Abelian sandpile model, a self-organized-critical lattice model of grains that topple when a site exceeds a threshold, studied on the upper half-plane with open or closed boundaries and compared to a c=-2 logarithmic theory.
brief_reason: The exact lattice-probability computation plus identification of observables with (logarithmic) CFT operators and their scaling/correlation forms is the transferable machinery, while the sandpile height variables and their self-organized-critical toppling dynamics are the domain-specific object.
